<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> White theological assumptions for a great swath of Christians in the mid-20th century, and unfortunately for some today, reinforce the status quo of alike saying things like, "I went to my first party in<|5.90|> <|5.90|> over a year and I didn't know what to say or where to hold my hands or how even to stand<|11.46|> <|11.46|> next to other people."<|12.94|> <|12.94|> Y'all remember that?<|16.96|> <|16.96|> When that which is rare suddenly becomes common, it takes time to adjust to its presence.<|27.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> What am I to do with this voice saying, "Samuel, Samuel,"<|9.00|> <|9.00|> the boy prophet might have asked Eli.<|13.00|> <|13.00|> God's intimate overture towards Samuel was chasmed by its rarity.<|21.00|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Imagination, Eli seems to tell him, bridges that chasm,<|5.00|> <|6.44|> marrying his
